title: Addition of the adaptor to RNA substrates for 3′ RACE
author: Matus Valach
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xsyfnfw

steps.instruction: Mix the following components (9 μL): %s %s Blocking the 3′ end of the 3′ RACE oligo is not necessary, as 3′-OH ends are not a substrate for EcRtcB RNA ligase. 5′-end has to be hydroxylated. For practical purposes, a 5′ RACE RNA oligo, which usually has both ends hydroxylated, can be used for as the 3′ RACE oligo when mapping 3′-P ends by EcRtcB.
Denature for 2 min at 70 °C, place on ice.
Purify the RNA from the RNA ligase reaction (e.g., trizol extraction or column clean-up).

Proceed to RT-PCR. For the RT reaction, use 250-500 ng of the purified adaptor-ligated RNA and a primer reverse complementary to the 3′ adaptor. For the PCR, use an upstream (forward) primer binding to the RNA of interest and a downstream (reverse) primer binding to the 3′ adaptor.
